facts: The Moon's gravity stabilizes Earth's axis, which is tilted at 23.5 degrees, roughly the length of 4,700 football fields
Without the Moon, Earth's rotation would slow by 1.8 seconds per century, resulting in 12.5% longer days
The increased distance between the Earth and the Sun would be about 370,000 kilometers, roughly the distance from New York to Los Angeles and back
idea.payoff: and we still don't know how this would impact our planet's ability to support life
idea.title: 🌕 No Moon?! 🌎
idea.description: Imagine a world without the Moon's gentle glow 🌕. The consequences would be staggering, from drastically altered tides to a slower rotation period 🌎. Would our planet still be able to support life as we know it? 🤔 Watch to find out what a Moonless Earth would look like and how it would change our world forever 🌟

scenes.visual: View of Earth from space, with ocean currents visible. The Moon is conspicuously absent. A visual representation of reduced tidal movement (e.g., water level decreasing slightly).
Earth rotating in space with a clear 23.5-degree axial tilt highlighted by an infographic line. A subtle 'wobble' effect is contained, implying the Moon's stabilizing force.
Earth slowly rotating, transitioning from a normal rotation speed to a visibly slower one. A digital clock icon in the corner shows time passing slower.

scenes.text:
Earth-Sun distance increases 370,000km.
NYC to LA & back!
scenes.visual: Earth shown orbiting the Sun, then gradually moving further away. A dashed line illustrates the increased distance, with small, abstract icons of New York and Los Angeles appearing to visualize the comparative distance.

scenes.text:
How would this impact life?
We still don't know.
scenes.visual: A dramatic shot of Earth, perhaps showing extreme weather patterns, barren landscapes, or a turbulent atmosphere, with a large, ominous question mark overlaid. Emphasizes uncertainty.
